properties.title: How AI Will Change Quantum Computing - Ep. 294

properties.label: AI in quantum computing
properties.description: AI is seen as a key technology to unlock progress and breakthroughs in quantum computing, particularly in areas like error correction and discovering new applications.

properties.label: Quantum error correction
properties.description: A crucial process for building useful quantum hardware, it involves correcting errors in qubits without directly observing them, which is a complex challenge.

properties.label: Quantum computing basics
properties.description: Quantum computing uses quantum mechanical objects as switches (qubits) instead of transistors, enabling it to solve problems intractable for classical computers.

properties.label: Qubit properties
properties.description: Qubits can exist in a superposition of states (0 and 1) and are very fragile, easily losing their quantum information if disturbed.

properties.label: Quantum hardware development
properties.description: Building quantum processors (QPUs) is a new kind of hardware endeavor with many different approaches to creating qubits, making standardization challenging.

properties.label: Quantum computing applications
properties.description: Potential applications include pharmaceuticals, materials development, financial services, and logistics, with early benefits expected in simulating quantum systems like molecules.

properties.label: NVIDIA AI podcast
properties.description: A podcast discussing advancements in artificial intelligence, featuring guests from the tech industry.

properties.label: NVIDIA Ising models
properties.description: A family of open AI models specifically designed for quantum computing workloads, including calibration and decoding for quantum error correction.

properties.label: Scaling quantum hardware
properties.description: A major challenge is scaling up quantum systems to millions of qubits, which is necessary for tasks like error correction and requires leveraging supercomputing advancements.

properties.label: Hybrid quantum-classical systems
properties.description: The future of quantum computing is expected to involve integration with classical supercomputing, where quantum processors augment existing frameworks.

properties.name: Nick Harrigan
properties.label: Nick Harrigan
properties.description: Product marketing manager for quantum computing at NVIDIA, featured as a guest on the podcast.

properties.name: Noah Kravitz
properties.label: Noah Kravitz
properties.description: Host of the NVIDIA AI podcast.
